rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VOLTAIRE B. ESTRADA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate will be set for 8/19/2025. The personal representative must file either petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 8/19/2025 if no petition has been filed. (This Court has not required the local form Declaration of Real Property since November 2020. A fiduciary or a proposed fiduciary should make and verify her own statements, not merely verify her attorney's statements.)

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF CLIFF LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Authorizing Payment Of Fees And Costs To Attorney For Guardian Of The Estate Of Cliff Lee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Item 2 of the proof of service filed on 6/20/24 is incomplete. (3) The first account does not appear to have been approved yet, as alleged in paragraphs 14 and 19 of the declaration filed on 6/14/24. (4) If time is billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (5) Some of the attorney costs appear to be quite high. The filing fee for the initial petition was $435, not $565, and the filing fee for the second account was $435, not $754. Why were most of the compliance documents not just mailed to the Court for filing? (6) The request for fees will be considered after the accountings are resolved. [LRSF 14.55 B.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/30/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FUMIKO TAKAGI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/4/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirements of Probate Code Sect. 11801, and assets distributable to the beneficiary qualify as a small estate, the original Sect. 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share must be filed before the hearing date. An affidavit filed pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100 must be filed conventionally. [LRSF 14.60 C.] (3) The proof of service was incomplete at item 4a (date of mailing). (4) The accounting is not a proper accounting. [Probate Code Sections 1061 and 1062.] (5) Only the financial institution statements verifying the assets on hand at the end of the accounting period are required to be filed, but the fiduciary's or attorney's statement regarding the retention of the original statement(s) is required to be filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator did not actually marshal assets until February 2024. The heirs should have been clarified at the outset of these proceedings in the manner in which they were finally set forth in this petition so that there was no doubt that any predeceased siblings were survived by issue.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE FRANCIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/10/24. (No earlier availability.) The following issue(s) posted for the hearing on 5/7/24 still have not been addressed: (1) The deadline for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any responsive papers was 7/19/24. A "response" by the attorney was filed on 7/23/24 (e-filed on 7/24/24), and no courtesy copy has been received by the examiner. (2) A proposed fiduciary must sign and verify her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to her petition. [CRC 7.103(c).] (A "response" to issues posted by an examiner is not an appropriate response: see CRC 7.3 re supplementing or amending a petition.) (3) Both the wife and the other daughter post-deceased. Until someone is appointed as the personal representative of each of those estates, this petitioner cannot be appointed. (4) Notice must be properly served on the personal representative(s) of the post-deceased heirs' estates. Deadline is 8/23/24 for providing a proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL J. TEDESCHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) When the proposed personal representative is also the trustee of the devisee trust, all of the beneficiaries of the trust must be listed, identified as such, and be given notice. (6) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/27/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES COTTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT WYATT HUNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit the will. No appearance required. Status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for 09/02/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRENDAN H. LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Notwithstanding the waivers of bond, a proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors. A declaration must be filed, setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate in order for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/20/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A decedent's siblings are not heirs when parents survived.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF UNG YEP LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Item 8 is missing the ages of the persons listed and does not clearly identify all of the trust beneficiaries. The granddaughter and grandson are presumably the children of the petitioners, as Petitioners allege that there are no issue of a predeceased child, but it is unclear why these grandchildren are listed. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/27/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
